prompt:
Given the case description, identify the MOST relevant CRISPR delivery method from the options below:

a. Plasmid Transfection
b. Lentivirus/Retrovirus
c. RNP/mRNA electroporation
d. RNP/mRNA microinjection
e. mRNA LNP
f. AAV

Category: Cell line
Case Description: I hope to edit HEK293T cell line

Please provide your response as follows:
- Most relevant method (select one letter a-f):

raw_response:
Thinking and reasoning:
HEK293T is a robust, fast-growing human embryonic kidney cell line that is highly amenable to chemical (lipid) or calcium phosphate plasmid transfection. For routine genome editing of a standard cell line like HEK293T, plasmid transfection (Cas9 and sgRNA plasmids) is commonly used, simple, cost-effective, and yields high editing rates in this line. Alternatives like RNP electroporation can give faster and cleaner edits with lower off-targets, but are more involved and not strictly necessary for HEK293T. Viral methods (lentivirus/AAV) are used for difficult-to-transfect cells or stable integration; microinjection and LNPs are not typical for standard HEK293T editing.

Plan:
1. [ ] Create a short checklist plan for deciding the best delivery method.
2. [ ] Evaluate HEK293T properties vs. delivery method pros/cons.
3. [ ] Provide the final selected option letter.

Step 1:
1. [✓] Create a short checklist plan for deciding the best delivery method (completed)

3. [ ] Provide the final selected option letter

<solution>a</solution>
